<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Or do we, in our own modest ways, as one person put it, suck up the air in every room?<|8.92|>
<|16.42|> If you step back from the profound details of this gospel reading...<|22.30|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> There's wonderful good news for all of us.<|5.00|>
<|6.80|> Lent is not about trying harder.<|9.98|>
<|11.70|> Our Savior is with us.<|14.90|>
<|16.22|> And in fact, he is the one who goes first.<|19.52|>

<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And he goes on up ahead of us and he clears a path for us and he comes back to get us when we get scared or lonely or we can't take the next step to travel with us.<|12.88|>
<|13.98|> And I am certain in all of our prayers throughout this Lenten season promise that when we do succumb to any one of these temptations or many more...<|24.70|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> that Jesus offers us, like God, forgiveness.<|4.16|>
<|5.78|> In a nutshell, Jesus is the one who goes on up ahead of us.<|11.38|>
<|12.10|> Jesus is the one who travels with us.<|14.50|>
<|15.78|> And Jesus is, I believe, the one who follows us<|20.10|>
<|20.10|> even more than we follow him.<|23.24|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Amen.<|2.00|><|endoftext|>
